name: godot-rust (gdext)
description: Official Rust bindings for Godot 4; drive the mature Godot engine and editor with Rust gameplay code

kind.comment: Rust bindings (via GDExtension) that let you write gameplay logic in Rust for the Godot 4 engine; Rust is a first-class scripting/gameplay language over a non-Rust engine.

license.comment: The gdext binding is licensed MPL-2.0. The underlying Godot engine itself is MIT-licensed.

documentation.comment: Official godot-rust book (mdBook); API docs at docs.rs/godot.

first-release.comment: gdext was open-sourced as experimental Godot 4 bindings in November 2022; the `godot` crate's first real gdext release (0.1.1) was published to crates.io in June 2024.

latest-stable-version.comment: 0.5.4 published to crates.io 2026-06-23; verified 2026-07-06. Still pre-1.0.

maintenance-status.comment: Latest release 2026-06-23; regular releases and active development led by Jan Haller (Bromeon).

maturity.comment: Binding is pre-1.0 (0.5.x) with periodic breaking changes and migration guides, though it drives the fully mature Godot 4 engine. Anchored to the binding's API churn per the guide.

backing.value: Community (godot-rust organization; lead maintainer Jan Haller / Bromeon) + GitHub Sponsors
backing.comment: Community open-source project under the godot-rust org, funded via sponsorships/donations; Godot itself is stewarded by the Godot Foundation.

implementation.comment: Binds to the C++ Godot 4 engine through the GDExtension C API; the engine (rendering, physics, audio, editor) is not Rust.

architecture.comment: Inherits Godot's node/scene-tree model; dominant model is the scene graph of object-oriented nodes. Not ECS.

built-in-ecs.comment: Godot is node-based, not ECS; per the guide, node-based engines driven via bindings are false.

dimensionality.comment: Godot 4 has full first-party 2D and 3D tooling; both are exposed through the bindings.

scripting.comment: gdext itself makes Rust the gameplay language; within the same Godot project you can additionally use GDScript and C# alongside Rust.

data-driven-scenes.comment: Godot scenes (.tscn/.scn) are authored in the editor, serialized to files, and loaded at runtime; Rust classes can be attached to nodes.

reflection-serialization.comment: Rust classes register properties with Godot's runtime object/property system; Godot Resources serialize to disk, and a `serde` feature is available.

hot-reload.comment: Since Godot 4.2, GDExtensions hot-reload: recompiled Rust code is picked up without restarting the editor (use the `reloadable` setting; cargo watch to auto-rebuild).

visual-editor.comment: Uses Godot's full graphical scene/level editor; the editor comes from Godot, not the Rust binding.

live-inspector.comment: Godot editor's Inspector and remote scene tree show exported properties of Rust-registered classes at edit and run time; provided by Godot.

asset-pipeline.comment: Inherits Godot's full import/processing pipeline with reimport and a broad format set.

cli-project-tooling.comment: No dedicated gdext CLI or cargo plugin; projects are set up with Godot's own project manager plus a normal cargo crate. Community starter templates exist.

debug-profiling-tools.comment: Godot editor ships a built-in debugger/profiler and monitors; Rust-side debugging uses standard tooling (gdb/lldb).

ide-support.value: Godot editor for scenes/inspector; standard rust-analyzer for the Rust code
ide-support.comment: No dedicated Rust-in-Godot IDE plugin; rust-analyzer works out of the box and the Godot editor handles scenes.

rendering-backend.comment: Rendering is Godot's: Vulkan (Forward+/Mobile), Direct3D 12 and Metal backends, and an OpenGL/WebGL2 Compatibility renderer (used for web). Godot does not use wgpu.

lighting.comment: Godot 4 Forward+ is a clustered forward renderer; baked lightmaps plus realtime GI (SDFGI/VoxelGI) and full 2D lighting are available.

shadows.comment: Godot provides real-time shadow mapping (3D) and 2D shadows.

custom-shaders.comment: Godot shading language plus a visual shader graph.

sprites-tilemaps.comment: Godot ships Sprite2D batching and a first-party TileMap system.

particle-system.comment: Godot has built-in GPU/CPU particle systems for 2D and 3D.

post-processing.comment: Godot's WorldEnvironment provides glow/bloom, tonemapping, SSAO, etc.

text-rendering.comment: Godot has built-in font/text rendering and Label/RichTextLabel nodes.

physics.comment: Godot ships built-in 2D and 3D physics (Godot Physics; Jolt-based 3D in recent versions).

input.comment: Godot's input system supports keyboard/mouse, gamepad, and touch, exposed through the bindings.

animation.comment: Godot provides AnimationPlayer (incl. skeletal), AnimatedSprite (sprite-frame), Tween, and AnimationTree state machines.

networking.comment: Godot ships high-level multiplayer/replication (MultiplayerAPI) over ENet.

gltf-model-import.comment: Godot imports glTF/GLB (and other) 3D model formats.

macos.comment: Primary supported desktop platform (Apple Silicon + Intel).

web-wasm.comment: Web/WASM export is supported but marked experimental (gated behind the `experimental-wasm` feature); tooling is still maturing.

ios.comment: iOS is listed as supported but experimental in the repo; documentation and tooling are still in development.

android.comment: Android is listed as supported but experimental in the repo; documentation and tooling are still in development.

consoles.comment: No publicly reproducible first-party console path for gdext; Godot console ports are handled by third-party porting companies (e.g. W4 Games) and are not documented for the Rust bindings.

clean-build-time.comment: Not measured on a standard machine this pass. The first gdext build code-generates the whole Godot API, which is a known multi-minute cost; the guide requires a measured number, so left null.

incremental-build-time.comment: Not measured. Incremental rebuilds of gameplay code are generally fast once the binding codegen is cached, but no consistent measurement was taken this pass.

binary-size.comment: Not measured. The Rust GDExtension library is separate from the Godot runtime/export template (~tens of MB), so a single stripped-binary figure isn't directly comparable; left null.

web-bundle-size.comment: Not measured. Godot web exports (engine wasm + pck) are large, and gdext web support is experimental; no reliable current post-wasm-opt figure available.

runtime-overhead.comment: Not measured; the guide says to prefer null over a guess for idle CPU/memory.

github-stars.comment: GitHub displays the count as "5k" (approximate); snapshot 2026-07-06.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the top-level `godot` crate as of 2026-07-06.

shipped-titles.comment: No widely documented commercial titles are known to use the Rust bindings specifically. Godot itself has shipped many games, but those overwhelmingly use GDScript/C#, not gdext.

community-size.comment: Dedicated godot-rust Discord and regular contributions; smaller than Godot's own or Bevy's community.

plugin-asset-ecosystem.comment: A growing set of Rust crates/helpers around gdext; Godot's own AssetLib is large but is overwhelmingly GDScript addons rather than Rust plugins.

learning-resources.comment: Official godot-rust book, API docs, in-repo examples, and a growing set of tutorials/videos; not yet the volume of Godot's own (GDScript-focused) learning ecosystem.
